canonical_question: What are the Salesforce REST API capabilities, rate limits, and governor limits by edition?
aliases: Salesforce API rate limits by edition
Salesforce API call limits Enterprise vs Unlimited
Salesforce Composite API limits and capabilities
How many API calls does Salesforce allow per day?

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: v61.0 (Summer '24) - Deprecated SOAP API for new managed packages

constraints: Enterprise edition: 100,000 base API calls/24h + 1,000 per Salesforce/Platform user license
Unlimited edition: 100,000 base API calls/24h + 5,000 per Salesforce/Platform user license
Developer edition: 15,000 total API calls/24h (no per-user additions)
sObject Collections: max 200 records per create/update/upsert/delete request
Composite API: max 25 subrequests per composite request
Composite Graph: max 500 subrequests per request
SOQL query results: max 2,000 records per page, 50,000 per transaction
Bulk API 2.0: 15,000 batch submissions per 24h, 150 MB per file
Governor limits are per-transaction -- triggers cascade from the same pool

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs to move >10,000 records in batch/ETL operations
User needs real-time event notifications or webhooks
User needs to manipulate metadata (custom objects, fields, layouts)

related_to.label: Salesforce Platform Events & Change Data Capture

often_confused_with.label: Bulk API 2.0 (different rate pool, async, CSV-based)

inputs_needed.question: What integration pattern do you need?
Which Salesforce edition are you using?
